- [dream-mines-decision-corpus](dream-mines-decision-corpus.md) `convention` scope=`skills/woostack-dream/**` — woostack-dream mines specs/plans/fixes for recurring design decisions.
- [fix-delegates-to-execute](fix-delegates-to-execute.md) `convention` scope=`skills/woostack-fix/**` — woostack-fix delegates execution to woostack-execute (the fix file IS the plan); never re-inline a TDD/commit/distill loop.
- [in-woostack-dream-skill-md-knowledge-store-umbrella-memory-s](in-woostack-dream-skill-md-knowledge-store-umbrella-memory-s.md) `convention` scope=`skills/woostack-dream/**` — In woostack-dream SKILL.md, "knowledge store" (umbrella: memory store + tracked docs) and "memory store" (.woostack/memory/ only) are intentional distinct terms — do not flag as terminology drift.
- [memory-store-scoped-only](memory-store-scoped-only.md) `convention` scope=`skills/woostack-*/**,skills/woostack-init/**` — Memory tooling uses only the scoped store; absent store means skip/empty.
- [memory-telemetry-sidecar](memory-telemetry-sidecar.md) `convention` scope=`skills/woostack-init/scripts/**` — Recall telemetry belongs in .telemetry.tsv, not note frontmatter.
- [plan-artifact-quotes-target-relative-links](plan-artifact-quotes-target-relative-links.md) `convention` scope=`.woostack/fixes/**,.woostack/plans/**` — A plan/fix artifact quoting another file's relative link renders 'broken' from its own dir but is correct for the target — don't flag it.
- [review-action-trigger-gates](review-action-trigger-gates.md) `convention` scope=`.github/workflows/**` — Review-trigger workflows must actor+phrase gate issue_comment and avoid automatic secret-dependent fork PR runs.
- [review-add-angle-sites](review-add-angle-sites.md) `convention` scope=`skills/woostack-review/**` — Adding a woostack-review angle touches 11 sites — the easy-to-miss four are load-config VALID_ANGLES, the anthropic.md tier list, the _header footer whitelist, and the committed gating test.
- [skill-description-angle-bracket-placeholders](skill-description-angle-bracket-placeholders.md) `convention` scope=`skills/**/SKILL.md` — Angle-bracket placeholders like `<plan-path>` in a SKILL.md `description:` are an accepted, shipped convention here — not an XML/HTML defect; the review `skills` angle must not flag them.
- [skill-test-assert-ascii-token](skill-test-assert-ascii-token.md) `convention` scope=`skills/**/scripts/tests/*.sh` — Grep-based skill tests should assert an ASCII token, not a unicode literal (e.g. a → arrow); keep the readable unicode in the prose and assert on an ASCII phrase that lives in the same text.
- [tracked-memory-rides-increment-commit](tracked-memory-rides-increment-commit.md) `convention` scope=`skills/woostack-execute/**,skills/woostack-init/references/**` — Tracked memory notes are written in the increment worktree and committed with it.

- [woostack-command-surface-bookkeeping](woostack-command-surface-bookkeeping.md) `convention` scope=`AGENTS.md,README.md,CONTRIBUTING.md,skills/using-woostack/**` — Adding/removing a public command means updating the count + lists in five places, which drift independently.
- [woostack-commit-template-fence-trailer](woostack-commit-template-fence-trailer.md) `convention` scope=`skills/woostack-commit/SKILL.md` — woostack-commit PR-body template must keep ONLY the clean Spec: .woostack/specs/<file>.md line inside the fenced block; document the fixes/ trailer variant in prose outside the fence — agents emit the fence literally, so any parenthetical/annotation inside it breaks the status exact-match. Do not re-flag the template-vs-rules 'inconsistency'.
- [woostack-feature-state-invariant](woostack-feature-state-invariant.md) `convention` scope=`.woostack/specs/**,.woostack/plans/**,skills/woostack-status/**` — Specs, plans, and increment PRs are joined by Source and Spec trailers.
- [woostack-review-is-not-stack-aware-224-a-skill-add-pr-may-de](woostack-review-is-not-stack-aware-224-a-skill-add-pr-may-de.md) `convention` scope=`AGENTS.md,README.md,skills/using-woostack/**,skills/*/SKILL.md` — woostack-review is not stack-aware (#224): a skill-add PR may defer command-surface registration (routing/AGENTS.md/README) to a stacked follow-up PR — accept the 'missing from routing/surface lists' finding when a later stacked PR does the wiring. The lockstep sites themselves are in [[woostack-command-surface-bookkeeping]]; example split: PR #268 (skill) + #269 (wiring).
- [authored-mdx-escapes-jsx-and-table-pipes](authored-mdx-escapes-jsx-and-table-pipes.md) `gotcha` scope=`site/content/docs/**` — In a hand-authored site/content/docs/*.mdx page, bare `{…}` and `<word>` parse as JSX and a literal `|` splits a GFM table cell — backtick placeholders/values and escape table pipes as `\|`, or `next build` fails.
- [bash-source-empty-under-zsh-source](bash-source-empty-under-zsh-source.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/scripts/**,skills/woostack-address-comments/scripts/**` — ${BASH_SOURCE[0]} is empty when a script is SOURCED from zsh — use ${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0} for self-path resolution.
- [concepts-landing-is-folder-index](concepts-landing-is-folder-index.md) `gotcha` scope=`site/content/docs/**` — The /docs/concepts landing + nav come from the concepts/ folder (its meta.json + index.mdx), NOT the same-named sibling concepts.mdx — that file is a pre-split orphan that shadow-collides on the route. Add concept-page cards to concepts/index.mdx; never to concepts.mdx.
- [delegated-review-no-silent-downgrade](delegated-review-no-silent-downgrade.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-sweep/**,skills/woostack-execute-overnight/**` — A delegated review/verify step written as bare prose — no execution receipt, no provenance on its `clean` verdict — lets an autonomous driver silently downgrade it to a cheap self-review and emit a false `clean`.
- [execute-authors-terminal-plan-done](execute-authors-terminal-plan-done.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-execute/**,skills/woostack-status/scripts/**` — woostack-execute authors exactly ONE frontmatter write — the terminal plan `status: done` at the final increment (all boxes `[x]`, plan files only; skipped for `.woostack/fixes/`). Authoring `done` pre-merge fixes file-rot but does NOT make the board show `done`: `resolve_phase` returns `in-review` whenever a PR is open.
- [execute-inline-task-review](execute-inline-task-review.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-execute/**` — Inline execute should reuse bounded task spec and quality checks instead of falling back to a broader PR review loop.
- [fumadocs-folder-index-title-duplicates-nav](fumadocs-folder-index-title-duplicates-nav.md) `gotcha` scope=`site/content/docs/**,site/components/concepts/**` — Fumadocs shows folder meta title and index page title separately, so matching names create duplicate parent/child labels.
- [gate-needs-hard-barrier](gate-needs-hard-barrier.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/**/SKILL.md` — A load-bearing approval gate written only as soft body prose gets skipped by low-effort/fast models; make it a prominent STOP barrier AND restate it in Hard constraints.
- [gh-search-fuzzy-trailer-match](gh-search-fuzzy-trailer-match.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-status/scripts/**` — `gh pr list --search` tokenizes paths and cross-matches; exact-match the trailer in the PR body.
- [grep-assertion-single-physical-line](grep-assertion-single-physical-line.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/**` — A grep plan-assertion only matches within one physical line; reflow inserted prose so each asserted phrase is unbroken, and write the test one-assertion-per-line (no backticks / `&&\` continuations).
- [grep-c-counts-lines-not-occurrences](grep-c-counts-lines-not-occurrences.md) `gotcha` scope=`.woostack/plans/**` — A plan's `grep -c` verification counts matching LINES, not occurrences — a phrase that line-wraps or two patterns sharing a line undercount.
- [headless-clack-cli-needs-trickle-cr](headless-clack-cli-needs-trickle-cr.md) `gotcha` scope=`site/**,skills/woostack-bootstrap/**` — Drive an interactive clack `create-*` CLI headlessly by trickle-feeding CR (`\r`) into its stdin pipe with delays — not piped `\n`, not expect text-matching.
- [memory-source-field-multi-reader](memory-source-field-multi-reader.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-doctor/scripts/checks/memory*.sh,skills/woostack-init/references/memory.md` — A memory note's frontmatter source: is parsed by TWO doctor checks (provenance + unresolved-link) — change its form and both must move, and the prefix set is specs|plans|fixes.
- [review-angle-trigger-precision](review-angle-trigger-precision.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/**` — Enriching a diff-gated angle's prompt is dead unless detect-angles.sh also matches the new pattern — and never broaden a trigger on a common token.
- [review-config-bool-jq-default](review-config-bool-jq-default.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/scripts/**` — jq `// default` silently coerces an explicit `false` to the default — wrong for any default-TRUE config bool.
- [review-final-findings-need-postable-anchors](review-final-findings-need-postable-anchors.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/scripts/**` — Review validator agreement does not prove GitHub-postable anchors; final `findings.json` must be filtered against the current PR file set and `resolve-diff-line.sh` after intersection/classification.
- [review-host-distinct-from-model-provider](review-host-distinct-from-model-provider.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/**,action.yml` — woostack-review's CLI host and model provider are separate layers — google.md is named per provider but its body is host orchestration. Migrate one without touching the other.
- [review-incremental-three-dot-off-pr](review-incremental-three-dot-off-pr.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/scripts/**` — a rebased branch makes the incremental marker a non-ancestor of HEAD, so the three-dot compare returns HTTP 200 with the rebased-over (off-PR) commits — NOT a 404 — and the 404-only fallback misses it; intersect the incremental diff against meta.json .files before it reaches workers/posting, or off-PR paths 422 at POST .../reviews.
- [review-marker-trust-asymmetry](review-marker-trust-asymmetry.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/scripts/**` — the incremental SHA watermark is WRITTEN in CI and local runs alike, but its read-side trust gate was bot-author-only (CI-shaped) — so a local re-review never trusted the marker it wrote; widen to bot OR (local-run AND author==self), gated on not-in-CI to keep forge-safety.
- [review-model-resolution-two-paths](review-model-resolution-two-paths.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/**` — woostack-review resolves the tier→model on two paths (CI single-session and local per-call); a config-aware resolver wired into only one silently regresses the other.
- [review-openai-tier-effort-override](review-openai-tier-effort-override.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/**` — OpenAI reasoning effort defaults (medium for deep, xhigh for standard/fast) must be configured in prompts, action.yml, and default_model_for.
- [review-prefetch-findings-guard-ci-gated](review-prefetch-findings-guard-ci-gated.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/scripts/**` — prefetch.sh's in-flight-findings.* guard must stay GITHUB_ACTIONS-gated — CI's validate job pre-downloads findings.* then re-runs prefetch, so warn-and-PRESERVE is load-bearing there; only the local branch may HARD-STOP. And the local default OUTDIR must be per-RUN, not per-project.
- [review-prompt-self-contained-blob](review-prompt-self-contained-blob.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/**` — woostack-review prompts ship as ONE self-contained blob to CI runners that follow no markdown links — shared content must be inlined, not just linked.
- [review-skip-markdown-only-pr](review-skip-markdown-only-pr.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/scripts/**` — prefetch.sh emits skip=true "no code files changed" on a markdown-only PR — a false-negative in this all-markdown skills repo; force the swarm on docs/skills angles instead of trusting the skip.
- [site-build-in-worktree-needs-real-node-modules](site-build-in-worktree-needs-real-node-modules.md) `gotcha` scope=`site/**` — Building site/ in a woostack worktree needs a real `pnpm install` — Turbopack rejects a node_modules symlink that escapes the worktree root.
- [skill-description-colon-space](skill-description-colon-space.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/*/SKILL.md` — A `word: ` colon-space in a SKILL.md description is a YAML mapping indicator; it throws a ScannerError and the installer silently skips the skill.
- [source-line-is-multi-reader-contract](source-line-is-multi-reader-contract.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-status/scripts/**,skills/woostack-doctor/scripts/checks/spec-plan-backlink.sh,skills/woostack-plan/**,skills/woostack-build/SKILL.md,skills/woostack-commit/SKILL.md` — The plan **Source:** line is a multi-reader contract — change its format and you must touch both parser regexes plus every authoring/contract doc, and keep readers back-compatible with on-disk path-form plans.
- [spec-template-md-html-mirror](spec-template-md-html-mirror.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-build/references/**` — spec-template.html's {{tokens}} are illustrative, not a substitution engine — woostack-visualize composes bespoke HTML; edit the .md and .html together and keep sections 1:1.
- [status-ready-phase-pr-not-drift](status-ready-phase-pr-not-drift.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-status/scripts/**` — The lag-flag head-state list must EXCLUDE any phase where a PR is legitimately expected (ready opens the spec+plan PR) — and mkplan defaults the plan to `planning`, so a "ready" test that omits the status arg never tests ready.
- [subagent-self-pins-to-worktree](subagent-self-pins-to-worktree.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-execute/**,skills/woostack-init/references/**` — Subagent isolation can't rely on a controller-set cwd; the dispatch prompt must make the implementer self-pin to $wt.
- [woostack-paths-anchor-to-repo-root](woostack-paths-anchor-to-repo-root.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/scripts/**,skills/woostack-address-comments/scripts/**` — Anchor every default `.woostack/` path to the git repo root via resolve-root.sh — never `$(pwd)` or a bare relative path.
- [worktree-local-state-common-root](worktree-local-state-common-root.md) `gotcha` scope=`skills/woostack-review/scripts/**,skills/woostack-address-comments/scripts/**,skills/woostack-init/references/**` — Worktree helpers need two roots: active checkout for tracked edits, primary/common checkout for local-only memory, wisdom, metrics, and telemetry sidecars.
- [fanout-empty-needs-receipt](fanout-empty-needs-receipt.md) `pattern` scope=`skills/woostack-review/**` — An empty aggregate from a fan-out of workers is ambiguous (clean vs never-ran) — require a per-worker execution receipt to disambiguate.
